sentences_input.text: But what happened is that perhaps among the six million, we lost, as a Jew, I can say that we lost more because we lost the children, the main factor that can reproduce and replace the values of Judaistic studies and Judaistic existence.

Not necessarily they have to be very religious or pay attention to this traditional life, but the base on this what we expect of the world is to deliver and to continue in good faith of delivering happiness among people in throughout the world, despite the fact that the world is still providing many ethnic groups and many people of destructive weapons and destructive forces for personal gains.

[PAUSES FOR 4 SECONDS] There are times where I recollect when I speak to youth groups and try to provide them the-- with the atrocities done to people as ethnic group, and not to permit the-- even for a moment, to separate or to point out that other ethnic groups, or races, or whatever it is of a different structure.
Every human being deserves just being a human being, deserves to be on this earth and experience a better condition to continue in the life.
And it is up to them to either to make it good or to lose the faith.

No, I'm fortunately not, I was fortunate enough that-- that right of liberation, after I [INAUDIBLE] left the hospital in [INAUDIBLE] and returned to the main camp in Mathausen, there was a Jewish doctor who encountered and was very much interested in finding youngsters of Jewish faith and talk to them.
And he mentioned to us that in case we having encountered any of the such conditions as self-blame, or that we survive and the others had to perish, is not the solution.
